briefdescription.para: unsigned 8-bit
signed 8-bit
unsigned 16-bit
signed 16-bit
unsigned 32-bit
signed 32-bit
32-bit floating point
64-bit double precision FP
boolean (XTRUE or XFALSE)
See the detailed description of the subtests in the file description.
This data type defines an interrupt handler for a device.

detaileddescription.para: The argument points to the instance of the component

briefdescription.para: This data type defines an exception handler for a processor.

detaileddescription.para: The argument points to the instance of the component

briefdescription.para: This data type defines a callback to be invoked when an assert occurs.

detaileddescription.para: The callback is invoked only when asserts are enabled

briefdescription.para: A structure that contains a time stamp used by other time stamp macros defined below.

detaileddescription.para: This structure is processor dependent.

briefdescription.para: A structure that contains a time stamp used by other time stamp macros defined below.

detaileddescription.para: This structure is processor dependent.

briefdescription.para: This variable allows testing to be done easier with asserts.

detaileddescription.para: An assert sets this variable such that a driver can evaluate this variable to determine if an assert occurred.

briefdescription.para: This variable allows the assert functionality to be changed for testing such that it does not wait infinitely.

detaileddescription.para: Use the debugger to disable the waiting during testing of asserts.

briefdescription.para: This variable allows testing to be done easier with asserts.

detaileddescription.para: An assert sets this variable such that a driver can evaluate this variable to determine if an assert occurred.

briefdescription.para: Implements assert.

detaileddescription.para: Currently, it calls a user-defined callback function if one has been set. Then, it potentially enters an infinite loop depending on the value of the XWaitInAssert variable.

parameterdescription.para: is the name of the filename of the source
is the linenumber within File

briefdescription.para: Sets up a callback function to be invoked when an assert occurs.

detaileddescription.para: If there was already a callback installed, then it is replaced.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback to be invoked when an assert is taken

simplesect.para: This function has no effect if NDEBUG is set

briefdescription.para: Null handler function.

detaileddescription.para: This follows the XInterruptHandler signature for interrupt handlers. It can be used to assign a null handler (a stub) to an interrupt controller vector table.

parameterdescription.para: is an arbitrary void pointer and not used.

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 *Addr, u32 Words, u32 Pattern, u8 Subtest)
memberdef.name: XUtil_MemoryTest32

briefdescription.para: Performs a destructive 32-bit wide memory test.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the region of memory to be tested.
is the length of the block.
is the constant used for the constant pattern test, if 0, 0xDEADBEEF is used.
is the test selected. See xutil.h for possible values.

listitem.para: XST_MEMTEST_FAILED is returned for a failure
XST_SUCCESS is returned for a pass

detaileddescription.para: Used for spaces where the address range of the region is smaller than the data width. If the memory range is greater than 2 ** width, the patterns used in XUT_WALKONES and XUT_WALKZEROS will repeat on a boundary of a power of two making it more difficult to detect addressing errors. The XUT_INCREMENT and XUT_INVERSEADDR tests suffer the same problem. Ideally, if large blocks of memory are to be tested, break them up into smaller regions of memory to allow the test patterns used not to repeat over the region tested.

memberdef.argsstring: (u16 *Addr, u32 Words, u16 Pattern, u8 Subtest)
memberdef.name: XUtil_MemoryTest16

briefdescription.para: Performs a destructive 16-bit wide memory test.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the region of memory to be tested.
is the length of the block.
is the constant used for the constant pattern test, if 0, 0xDEADBEEF is used.
is the test selected. See xutil.h for possible values.

listitem.para: XST_MEMTEST_FAILED is returned for a failure
XST_SUCCESS is returned for a pass

detaileddescription.para: Used for spaces where the address range of the region is smaller than the data width. If the memory range is greater than 2 ** width, the patterns used in XUT_WALKONES and XUT_WALKZEROS will repeat on a boundary of a power of two making it more difficult to detect addressing errors. The XUT_INCREMENT and XUT_INVERSEADDR tests suffer the same problem. Ideally, if large blocks of memory are to be tested, break them up into smaller regions of memory to allow the test patterns used not to repeat over the region tested.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 *Addr, u32 Words, u8 Pattern, u8 Subtest)
memberdef.name: XUtil_MemoryTest8

briefdescription.para: Performs a destructive 8-bit wide memory test.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the region of memory to be tested.
is the length of the block.
is the constant used for the constant pattern test, if 0, 0xDEADBEEF is used.
is the test selected. See xutil.h for possible values.

listitem.para: XST_MEMTEST_FAILED is returned for a failure
XST_SUCCESS is returned for a pass

detaileddescription.para: Used for spaces where the address range of the region is smaller than the data width. If the memory range is greater than 2 ** width, the patterns used in XUT_WALKONES and XUT_WALKZEROS will repeat on a boundary of a power of two making it more difficult to detect addressing errors. The XUT_INCREMENT and XUT_INVERSEADDR tests suffer the same problem. Ideally, if large blocks of memory are to be tested, break them up into smaller regions of memory to allow the test patterns used not to repeat over the region tested.

briefdescription.para: Rotates the provided value to the left one bit position.

detaileddescription.para:
Input
is value to be rotated to the left
Width
is the number of bits in the input data
The resulting unsigned long value of the rotate left
None.

briefdescription.para: Determines if the specified version is valid.

detaileddescription.para:
StringPtr
points to the string to be validated.
TRUE if the version string is a valid format, FALSE otherwise.
None.

briefdescription.para: Unpacks a packed version into the specified version.

detaileddescription.para: Versions are packed into the configuration ROM to reduce the amount storage. A packed version is a binary format as opposed to a non-packed version which is implemented as a string.

parameterdescription.para: points to the version to unpack the packed version into.
contains the packed version to unpack.

briefdescription.para: Packs a version into the specified packed version.

detaileddescription.para: Versions are packed into the configuration ROM to reduce the amount storage.

InstancePtr
points to the version to pack.
PackedVersionPtr
points to the packed version which will receive the new packed version.
A status, XST_SUCCESS, indicating the packing was accomplished successfully, or an error, XST_INVALID_VERSION, indicating the specified input version was not valid such that the pack did not occur
The packed version pointed to by PackedVersionPtr is modified with the new packed version if the status indicates success.
None.

briefdescription.para: Determines if two versions are equal.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
points to the first version to be compared.
VersionPtr
points to a second version to be compared.
TRUE if the versions are equal, FALSE otherwise.
None.

briefdescription.para: Converts a version to a null terminated string.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
points to the version to convert.
StringPtr
points to the string which will be the result of the conversion. This does not need to point to a null terminated string as an input, but must point to storage which is an adequate amount to hold the result string.
The null terminated string is inserted at the location pointed to by StringPtr if the status indicates success.
It is necessary for the caller to have already allocated the storage to contain the string. The amount of memory necessary for the string is specified in the version header file.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a version from a null terminated string.

detaileddescription.para: Since the string may not be a format which is compatible with the version, an error could occur.

InstancePtr
points to the version which is to be initialized.
StringPtr
points to a null terminated string which will be converted to a version. The format of the string must match the version string format which is X.YYX where X = 0 - 9, YY = 00 - 99, Z = a - z.
A status, XST_SUCCESS, indicating the conversion was accomplished successfully, or XST_INVALID_VERSION indicating the version string format was not valid.
None.

briefdescription.para: Copies the contents of a version to another version.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
points to the version which is the source of data for the copy operation.
VersionPtr
points to another version which is the destination of the copy operation.
None.
None.

briefdescription.para: Xilinx NULL, TRUE and FALSE legacy support.

detaileddescription.para: Deprecated.

briefdescription.para: component has been initialized
component has been started
Return the most significant half of the 64 bit data type.

parameterdescription.para: is the 64 bit word.

simplesect.para: The upper 32 bits of the 64 bit word.

briefdescription.para: Return the least significant half of the 64 bit data type.

parameterdescription.para: is the 64 bit word.

simplesect.para: The lower 32 bits of the 64 bit word.

memberdef.name: XASSERT_VOID
memberdef.initializer: { \ if (expression) \ { \ XAssertStatus = XASSERT_NONE; \ } \ else \ { \ XAssert(__FILE__, __LINE__); \ XAssertStatus = XASSERT_OCCURRED; \ return; \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: This assert macro is to be used for functions that do not return anything (void).

detaileddescription.para: This in conjunction with the XWaitInAssert boolean can be used to accommodate tests so that asserts which fail allow execution to continue.

parameterdescription.para: is the expression to evaluate. If it evaluates to false, the assert occurs.

simplesect.para: Returns void unless the XWaitInAssert variable is true, in which case no return is made and an infinite loop is entered.

memberdef.name: XASSERT_NONVOID
memberdef.initializer: { \ if (expression) \ { \ XAssertStatus = XASSERT_NONE; \ } \ else \ { \ XAssert(__FILE__, __LINE__); \ XAssertStatus = XASSERT_OCCURRED; \ return 0; \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: This assert macro is to be used for functions that do return a value.

detaileddescription.para: This in conjunction with the XWaitInAssert boolean can be used to accommodate tests so that asserts which fail allow execution to continue.

parameterdescription.para: is the expression to evaluate. If it evaluates to false, the assert occurs.

simplesect.para: Returns 0 unless the XWaitInAssert variable is true, in which case no return is made and an infinite loop is entered.

briefdescription.para: Always assert.

detaileddescription.para: This assert macro is to be used for functions that do not return anything (void). Use for instances where an assert should always occur.

simplesect.para: Returns void unless the XWaitInAssert variable is true, in which case no return is made and an infinite loop is entered.

briefdescription.para: Always assert.

detaileddescription.para: This assert macro is to be used for functions that do return a value. Use for instances where an assert should always occur.

simplesect.para: Returns void unless the XWaitInAssert variable is true, in which case no return is made and an infinite loop is entered.

briefdescription.para: Copies a non-overlapping block of memory.

parameterdescription.para: Destination address to copy data to.
Source address to copy data from.
Number of bytes to copy.

briefdescription.para: Fills an area of memory with constant data.

parameterdescription.para: Destination address to copy data to.
Value to set.
Number of bytes to copy.

briefdescription.para: Time is derived from the 64 bit PPC timebase register.

detaileddescription.para:
StampPtr
is the storage for the retrieved time stamp.
None.
Signature: void XENV_TIME_STAMP_GET(XTIME_STAMP *StampPtr)
This macro must be implemented by the user.

briefdescription.para: This macro is not yet implemented and always returns 0.

detaileddescription.para:
Stamp1Ptr
is the first sampled time stamp.
Stamp2Ptr
is the second sampled time stamp.
0
This macro must be implemented by the user.

briefdescription.para: This macro is not yet implemented and always returns 0.

detaileddescription.para:
Stamp1Ptr
is the first sampled time stamp.
Stamp2Ptr
is the second sampled time stamp.
0
This macro must be implemented by the user

briefdescription.para: Delay the specified number of microseconds.

parameterdescription.para: Number of microseconds to delay.

simplesect.para: XENV_USLEEP is deprecated. Use udelay() instead.

briefdescription.para: Copies a non-overlapping block of memory.

parameterdescription.para: Destination address to copy data to.
Source address to copy data from.
Number of bytes to copy.

simplesect.para: The use of XENV_MEM_COPY is deprecated. Use memcpy() instead.
This implementation MAY BREAK work if source and target memory area are overlapping.

briefdescription.para: Fills an area of memory with constant data.

parameterdescription.para: Destination address to copy data to.
Value to set.
Number of bytes to copy.

simplesect.para: The use of XENV_MEM_FILL is deprecated. Use memset() instead.

briefdescription.para: Time is derived from the 64 bit PPC timebase register.

detaileddescription.para:
StampPtr
is the storage for the retrieved time stamp.
None.
Signature: void XENV_TIME_STAMP_GET(XTIME_STAMP *StampPtr)
This macro must be implemented by the user.

briefdescription.para: This macro is not yet implemented and always returns 0.

detaileddescription.para:
Stamp1Ptr
is the first sampled time stamp.
Stamp2Ptr
is the second sampled time stamp.
0
This macro must be implemented by the user.

briefdescription.para: This macro is not yet implemented and always returns 0.

detaileddescription.para:
Stamp1Ptr
is the first sampled time stamp.
Stamp2Ptr
is the second sampled time stamp.
0
This macro must be implemented by the user.
Delay the specified number of microseconds. Not implemented without OS support.

parameterdescription.para: Number of microseconds to delay.

briefdescription.para: Copies a non-overlapping block of memory.

parameterdescription.para: Destination address to copy data to.
Source address to copy data from.
Number of bytes to copy.

simplesect.para: XENV_MEM_COPY is deprecated. Use memcpy() instead.

parameterdescription.para: Destination address to copy data to.
Value to set.

briefdescription.para: Time is derived from the 64 bit PPC timebase register.

detaileddescription.para:
StampPtr
is the storage for the retrieved time stamp.
None.
Signature: void XENV_TIME_STAMP_GET(XTIME_STAMP *StampPtr)

briefdescription.para: This macro is not yet implemented and always returns 0.

parameterdescription.para: is the first sampled time stamp.
is the second sampled time stamp.

briefdescription.para: This macro is not yet implemented and always returns 0.

detaileddescription.para:
Stamp1Ptr
is the first sampled time stamp.
Stamp2Ptr
is the second sampled time stamp.
0
None.

briefdescription.para: Delay the specified number of microseconds.

parameterdescription.para: Number of microseconds to delay.

memberdef.name: XPAR_SPI_0_SLAVE_ONLY
memberdef.initializer: FALSE /* Is the device slave only? */

memberdef.name: XPAR_SPI_1_SLAVE_ONLY
memberdef.initializer: FALSE /* Is the device slave only? */

memberdef.name: XPAR_FLASH_0_PART_WIDTH
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* Width of each part in bytes */

memberdef.name: XPAR_FLASH_0_PART_MODE
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* Mode of each part in bytes */

memberdef.name: XST_FIFO_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 7L /* an error occurred during an operation with a FIFO such as an underrun or overrun, this error requires the device to be reset */

memberdef.name: XST_RESET_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 8L /* an error occurred which requires the device to be reset */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 9L /* a DMA error occurred, this error typically requires the device using the DMA to be reset */

memberdef.name: XST_NOT_POLLED
memberdef.initializer: 10L /* the device is not configured for polled mode operation */

memberdef.name: XST_FIFO_NO_ROOM
memberdef.initializer: 11L /* a FIFO did not have room to put the specified data into */

memberdef.name: XST_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL
memberdef.initializer: 12L /* the buffer is not large enough to hold the expected data */

memberdef.name: XST_REGISTER_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 14L /* a register did not contain the expected value */

memberdef.name: XST_INVALID_PARAM
memberdef.initializer: 15L /* an invalid parameter was passed into the function */

memberdef.name: XST_NOT_SGDMA
memberdef.initializer: 16L /* the device is not configured for scatter-gather DMA operation */

memberdef.name: XST_NO_CALLBACK
memberdef.initializer: 18L /* a callback has not yet been registered */

memberdef.name: XST_NO_FEATURE
memberdef.initializer: 19L /* device is not configured with the requested feature */

memberdef.name: XST_NOT_INTERRUPT
memberdef.initializer: 20L /* device is not configured for interrupt mode operation */

memberdef.name: XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX
memberdef.initializer: 22L /* the error counters of a device have maxed out */

memberdef.name: XST_IS_STARTED
memberdef.initializer: 23L /* used when part of device is already started i.e. sub channel */

memberdef.name: XST_IS_STOPPED
memberdef.initializer: 24L /* used when part of device is already stopped i.e. sub channel */

memberdef.name: XST_NOT_ENABLED
memberdef.initializer: 29L /* a requested service is not available because it has not been enabled */

briefdescription.para: Event timeout occurred.
Used for feature deprecation.

memberdef.name: XST_PFIFO_BAD_REG_VALUE
memberdef.initializer: 503L /* self test, a register value was invalid after reset */

memberdef.name: XST_PFIFO_DEADLOCK
memberdef.initializer: 505L /* packet FIFO is reporting * empty and full simultaneously */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_RESET_REGISTER_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 512L /* self test, a register value was invalid after reset */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_SG_LIST_EMPTY
memberdef.initializer: 513L /* scatter gather list contains no buffer descriptors ready to be processed */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_SG_LIST_FULL
memberdef.initializer: 517L /* all the buffer descriptors of the scatter gather list are being used */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_SG_BD_LOCKED
memberdef.initializer: 518L /* the scatter gather buffer descriptor which is to be copied over in the scatter list is locked */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_SG_NOTHING_TO_COMMIT
memberdef.initializer: 519L /* no buffer descriptors have been put into the scatter gather list to be committed */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_SG_COUNT_EXCEEDED
memberdef.initializer: 521L /* the packet count threshold specified was larger than the total # of buffer descriptors in the scatter gather list */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_SG_LIST_EXISTS
memberdef.initializer: 522L /* the scatter gather list has already been created */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_SG_NO_LIST
memberdef.initializer: 523L /* no scatter gather list has been created */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_SG_BD_NOT_COMMITTED
memberdef.initializer: 524L /* the buffer descriptor which was being started was not committed to the list */

memberdef.name: XST_DMA_SG_NO_DATA
memberdef.initializer: 525L /* the buffer descriptor to start has already been used by the hardware so it can't be reused */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_REG_WIDTH_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 531L /* an invalid register width was passed into the function */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_RESET_REGISTER_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 532L /* the value of a register at reset was not valid */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_DEVICE_STATUS_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 533L /* a write to the device interrupt status register did not read back correctly */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_DEVICE_ACK_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 534L /* the device interrupt status register did not reset when acked */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_DEVICE_ENABLE_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 535L /* the device interrupt enable register was not updated when other registers changed */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_IP_STATUS_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 536L /* a write to the IP interrupt status register did not read back correctly */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_IP_ACK_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 537L /* the IP interrupt status register did not reset when acked */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_IP_ENABLE_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 538L /* IP interrupt enable register was not updated correctly when other registers changed */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_DEVICE_PENDING_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 539L /* The device interrupt pending register did not indicate the expected value */

memberdef.name: XST_IPIF_DEVICE_ID_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 540L /* The device interrupt ID register did not indicate the expected value */

memberdef.name: XST_EMAC_MEMORY_SIZE_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 1001L /* Memory space is not big enough * to hold the minimum number of * buffers or descriptors */

memberdef.name: XST_EMAC_MII_BUSY
memberdef.initializer: 1004L /* An MII operation is in progress */

memberdef.name: XST_EMAC_COLLISION_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 1007L /* Excess deferral or late * collision on polled send */

memberdef.name: XST_IIC_DTR_READBACK_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 1084 /* Read of the data Tx reg */

memberdef.name: XST_IIC_DRR_READBACK_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 1085 /* Read of the data Receive reg */

memberdef.name: XST_IIC_ADR_READBACK_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 1086 /* Read of the data Tx reg */

memberdef.name: XST_IIC_TBA_READBACK_ERROR
memberdef.initializer: 1087 /* Read of the 10 bit addr reg */

briefdescription.para: The device isn't a slave.
Arbitration lost for master.
This file contains basic functions for Xilinx software IP.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the interface for the XVersion component.
This file contains the implementation of the XVersion component.
Contains the memory test utility functions.
This file contains utility functions such as memory test functions.
This file contains Xilinx software status codes.
This file contains system parameters for the Xilinx device driver environment.
This is a legacy file kept for backwards compatibility.
Defines common services specified by xenv.h.
Defines common services that are typically found in a host operating.
This file contains basic types for Xilinx software IP.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a rpm 11/07/03 Added XNullHandler function as a stub interrupt handler 1.00a xd 11/03/04 Improved support for doxygen. 1.00a bss 13/01/12 Removed a compiler warning in XNullHandler;

detaileddescription.para: These types do not follow the standard naming convention with respect to using the component name in front of each name because they are considered to be primitives.
This file contains items which are architecture dependent.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a rmm 12/14/01 First release rmm 05/09/03 Added "xassert always" macros to rid ourselves of diab compiler warnings 1.00a rpm 11/07/03 Added XNullHandler function as a stub interrupt handler 1.00a rpm 07/21/04 Added XExceptionHandler typedef for processor exceptions 1.00a xd 11/03/04 Improved support for doxygen. 1.00a wre 01/25/07 Added Linux style data types u32, u16, u8, TRUE, FALSE 1.00a rpm 04/02/07 Added ifndef KERNEL around u32, u16, u8 data types

detaileddescription.para: environment. This include file simply includes an OS specific file based on the compile-time constant BUILD_ENV_*, where * is the name of the target environment.
All services are defined as macros.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b ch 10/24/02 Added XENV_LINUX 1.00a rmm 04/17/02 First release

simplesect.para: This file is not intended to be included directly by driver code. Instead, the generic xenv.h file is intended to be included by driver code.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wgr 02/28/07 Added cache handling macros. 1.00a wgr 02/27/07 Simplified code. Deprecated old-style macro names. 1.00a xd 11/03/04 Improved support for doxygen. 1.00a ch 10/24/02 First release 1.10a wgr 03/22/07 Converted to new coding style.

detaileddescription.para: Please modify your code to #include "xenv_standalone.h" instead.

simplesect.para: This file is not intended to be included directly by driver code. Instead, the generic xenv.h file is intended to be included by driver code.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wgr 02/28/07 Added cache handling macros. 1.00a wgr 02/27/07 Simplified code. Deprecated old-style macro names. 1.00a rmm 01/24/06 Implemented XENV_USLEEP. Assume implementation is being used under Xilinx standalone BSP. 1.00a xd 11/03/04 Improved support for doxygen. 1.00a rmm 03/21/02 First release 1.00a wgr 03/22/07 Converted to new coding style. 1.00a rpm 06/29/07 Added udelay macro for standalone 1.00a xd 07/19/07 Included xparameters.h as XPAR_ constants are referred to in MICROBLAZE section 1.00a ecm 09/19/08 updated for v7.20 of Microblaze, new functionality

simplesect.para: This file is not intended to be included directly by driver code. Instead, the generic xenv.h file is intended to be included by driver code.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a wgr 02/28/07 Added cache handling macros. 1.00a wgr 02/27/07 Simplified code. Deprecated old-style macro names. 1.00a xd 11/03/04 Improved support for doxygen. rmm 09/13/03 CR 177068: Fix compiler warning in XENV_MEM_FILL rmm 10/24/02 Added XENV_USLEEP macro 1.00a rmm 07/16/01 First release 1.10a wgr 03/22/07 Converted to new coding style.

detaileddescription.para: It is a representation of the system in that it contains the number of each device in the system as well as the parameters and memory map for each device. The user can view this file to obtain a summary of the devices in their system and the device parameters.
This file may be automatically generated by a design tool such as System Generator.
Status codes have their own data type called int. These codes are used throughout the Xilinx device drivers.

A subset of the memory tests can be selected or all of the tests can be run in order. If there is an error detected by a subtest, the test stops and the failure code is returned. Further tests are not run even if all of the tests are selected.
Subtest descriptions: XUT_ALLMEMTESTS: Runs all of the following tests

para.preformatted: XUT_INCREMENT: Incrementing Value Test. This test starts at 'XUT_MEMTEST_INIT_VALUE' and uses the incrementing value as the test value for memory.
XUT_WALKONES: Walking Ones Test. This test uses a walking '1' as the test value for memory. location 1 = 0x00000001 location 2 = 0x00000002 ...
XUT_WALKZEROS: Walking Zero's Test. This test uses the inverse value of the walking ones test as the test value for memory. location 1 = 0xFFFFFFFE location 2 = 0xFFFFFFFD ...
XUT_INVERSEADDR: Inverse Address Test. This test uses the inverse of the address of the location under test as the test value for memory.
XUT_FIXEDPATTERN: Fixed Pattern Test. This test uses the provided patterns as the test value for memory. If zero is provided as the pattern the test uses '0xDEADBEEF".

detaileddescription.para: The tests are DESTRUCTIVE. Run before any initialized memory spaces have been set up.

The address, Addr, provided to the memory tests is not checked for validity except for the NULL case. It is possible to provide a code-space pointer for this test to start with and ultimately destroy executable code causing random failures.

Used for spaces where the address range of the region is smaller than the data width. If the memory range is greater than 2 ** width, the patterns used in XUT_WALKONES and XUT_WALKZEROS will repeat on a boundary of a power of two making it more difficult to detect addressing errors. The XUT_INCREMENT and XUT_INVERSEADDR tests suffer the same problem. Ideally, if large blocks of memory are to be tested, break them up into smaller regions of memory to allow the test patterns used not to repeat over the region tested.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm 11/01/01 First release 1.00a xd 11/03/04 Improved support for doxygen.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm 11/01/01 First release 1.00a xd 11/03/04 Improved support for doxygen.

detaileddescription.para: This component represents a version ID. It is encapsulated within a component so that it's type and implementation can change without affecting users of it.
The version is formatted as X.YYZ where X = 0 - 9, Y = 00 - 99, Z = a - z X is the major revision, YY is the minor revision, and Z is the compatibility revision.
Packed versions are also utilized for the configuration ROM such that memory is minimized. A packed version consumes only 16 bits and is formatted as follows.

para.preformatted: Revision Range Bit Positions
Major Revision 0 - 9 Bits 15 - 12 Minor Revision 0 - 99 Bits 11 - 5 Compatibility Revision a - z Bits 4 - 0
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd 11/03/04 Improved support for doxygen.
